content.text:
NORTH CAROLINA 911 Standards Committee
Attendance
January 7, 2021
10:00 AM – Noon

elements.caption: Meeting Attendance
elements.summary: List of committee members and staff in attendance, indicating if present via Teams, in person, or not present.

content.text: Madam Chair called the meeting to order at 10:01 am

Mr. Bradford gave a legislative update. He spoke about the State legislature beginning their 
session January 13

. Mr. Bradford spoke about federal legislation specifically FCC fee diversion 
that was included in the updated CARES Act. Mr. Bradford shared a summary with Pokey and 
she displayed that summary on the screen. Mr. Bradford advised before, the FCC had no 
recourse to hold states accountable for fee diversions from the five states who did and this 
legislation will allow the FCC to create or adopt rules in 180 days related to fee diversion. Donna 
asked if these rules would affect NC and what is eligible for funding and Mr. Bradford advised at 
this time no. If legislation changed in NC then possibly, depending on the legislation. Donna 
spoke to one of the items on the review Mr. Bradford submitted specifically Colorado 
recognizing telecommunicators as first responders. Donna spoke to how that has affected some 
telecommunicators receiving the COVID vaccine. Mr. Bradford advised that certainly did have an 
immediate impact on telecommunicators. Pokey spoke about the states who have begun 
adopting telecommunicators at the state level as telecommunicators as this failed to gain a lot 
of ground federally.

Stephanie presented the AdHoc policy that was approved by the education committee and Mr. 
Bradford. No questions were asked on either the EMD or the Training portion of the policy. 
Dominick Nutter made the motion to approve the policy and Candy Miller seconded the motion. 
Motion passed unanimously.

Madam Chair then moved to priorities. A sub committee was created to address goal number 
three from the Standards Committee. Candy Miller, Dominick Nutter and Christy Shearin agreed 
to participate on the committee and will reach out to other PSAPs of varying sizes so there is fair 
and concise representation on the AdHoc committee. Candy Miller has agreed to be the chair of 
the AdHoc committee.

The conversation then moved to Peer Review. Tina and I will help to lead a committee to review 
the document and rules and will recruit PSAP Managers that are not currently in committees to 
assist. Madam Chair suggested the new supervisor in Moore County.

Finally, conversation was had on goal number two. This will encompass other committees as all 
will be needed to accomplish the goal. Gerry spoke on conversations he had with Rachel Bello 
and the possibility of a committee was discussed with a name of Next Gen or Emerging 
Technologies committee.

The Ad Hoc Policy was revisited. The original vote was not on the policy the Education 
Committee approved. Angie sent that to Stephanie and the policy was shared and reviewed. No 
questions were asked and the original vote was rescinded. Candy Miller made the motion to 
rescind the original vote and Dominick Nutter seconded the motion. Motion passed 
unanimously. A new motion was given to approve the present Ad Hoc policy from the Education 
Committee, Candy Miller made the motion, Dominick Nutter seconded the motion, and motion 
passed unanimously. No further conversation on topic.
Pokey wrapped up the policy conversation and Tine adjourned the meeting at 11:09.

subsections.title: 2021 Standards Committee Goals and Meeting Dates PDF

content.text:
Review and update potential verbiage of the Rules in order to prepare for the 
long process of updating them, focusing on the outcomes of legislative 
sessions.

Consider how a checks and balance would occur if there are new 
requirements regarding matters such as I3 standards and the need for 
collaboration between all committees regarding NG911 deployment.

Explore feasibility of establishing PSAP levels based on operational and 
technical capabilities.

Review and update verbiage of the policies relating to Peer Review to ensure 
consistent scheduling and review of PSAPs while considering options to 
accomplish them during the pandemic.

Hold joint meetings, twice annually at minimal, to review technological or 
operational changes that may need to be addressed in Standards or Peer 
Review.

Continue to explore feasibility of establishing PSAP levels based on 
operational and technical capabilities.
